prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Warhammer Fantasy Old World inspired grimdark low-fantasy RPG setting. Terrain type: dual_continent. Draw two large fictional continents in dark fantasy old-world cartography on aged parchment, separated by a dramatic non-vertical sea passage: diagonal and S-shaped storm straits, crescent gulfs, jagged narrows, shoals, broken coast shelves, and twisting channels rather than a simple straight split. One continent contains crumbling empire cities, river trading towns, witch-hunter roads, bleak farms, fortified passes, dwarf mountain holds, and dark forests; the other contains northern Chaos wastes, corrupted black forests, blighted marshes, plague coasts, greenskin borderlands, haunted barrows, cold steppe, and blasted badlands. Complex realistic coastlines on both continents: peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands, sea caves. Islands are unevenly scattered between and around the continents with varied size and shape, including cursed island chains, fortress isles, pirate coves, plague rocks, and storm reefs. Large detailed grimdark atlas, oppressive palette, sinister wilderness markers allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
